hero.headline: I build agent systems that do real work.
hero.sub: Voice agents that answer phones. An agent team that ships code. Pipelines that cut manual prospecting by 80%. I design multi-agent systems end-to-end and pick the stack per problem, not per habit.

about: I'm an AI engineering student at Esprit in Tunis. Most of what I build starts the same way: I notice work being done by hand that a system could do better, and I can't leave it alone. That's how the prospecting agents happened, and the recruitment pipelines, and even the little resume dashboard I built for myself when I got tired of re-editing my CV for every application.

I switch stacks often, on purpose. This field moves weekly, so I try new tools as they land, keep what earns its place, and drop what doesn't. The point is the most relevant solution for the use case, not the most familiar one.
Outside of code I'm a scout leader with the Tunisian Scouts and a member of JCI Kelibia.
